metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Co-locate the `exec == 'DET'` exclusion guard with `route_criteria`'s filter logic (orchestrator.py:~296): add an explicit `if str(c.get('exec','')).upper() == 'DET': continue` before the `exec_tier` agent-vs-single split, and note this in the Scope file list.
findings.finding: The DET-batch exclusion decision rule — `str(crit.get('exec','')).upper() == 'DET'` read directly from the descriptor dict, NOT via `exec_tier()` — is specified in the isolation section but is not co-located with the `route_criteria` function (orchestrator.py:268–301) or `exec_tier()` (registry.py:519–525). `route_criteria` currently filters only via `registry.exec_tier(c) == 'AGENT'`; `exec_tier()` provably remaps any `exec: DET` descriptor to `'1-TURN'`. The plan's decision protocol lives in a narrative section; the function that must implement it has no corresponding update described.
findings.location: 'DET-tier isolation' section (remediation, run 4) vs orchestrator.py:297 / registry.py:519–525

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a checkboxed AC: 'exec:DET descriptors are excluded from orchestrator.route_criteria and workflow_ops.plan_review_assemble_criteria; a test asserts an activated DET project criterion appears in DET-floor results and is absent from the LLM include_*/route_criteria set.'
findings.finding: The 'exec: DET LLM-batch exclusion' — that activated DET criteria are filtered out of orchestrator.route_criteria and workflow_ops.plan_review_assemble_criteria — is a described deliverable with a test assertion in the prose but no checkboxed acceptance criterion. If this exclusion is absent or broken, a prompt-less DET criterion will be handed to pass1_chunk and cause an error.
findings.location: DET-tier isolation, thin-slice sequencing & schema details — 'exec:DET criteria never enter the LLM batch' subsection; Acceptance Criteria sections (all rounds)
findings.suggested_fix: Promote the prose test assertion from 'DET-tier isolation' into a formal AC, e.g.: '- [ ] Activated exec:DET project criteria are never admitted to the LLM batch (orchestrator.route_criteria + plan_review_assemble_criteria both filter them); a test asserts the criterion appears in det_floor output and is absent from the include_* fan-out.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/code_review/criteria_routing.json` to the Scope file list, noting it must receive explicit `fail_mode: "closed"` and the `detector` selector shape for all existing security DET rows.
findings.finding: The Scope section lists only `code_review/detectors.py`, `plan_review/` (new wiring), `plan_review/registry.py`, and 'the routing schema' as files to modify — but the Schema+migration section explicitly states that `code_review/criteria_routing.json` must also be modified in this change (adding explicit `fail_mode: "closed"` to existing security rows and the new `detector` selector shape). An implementer following the Scope section's file list would miss this required data file edit.
findings.location: Scope section (file list) vs. 'Schema + migration details' section ('the built-in security rows in the packaged `code_review/criteria_routing.json` are written with an EXPLICIT `fail_mode: "closed"` in this change')
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, extend the file list: `src/rebar/llm/code_review/criteria_routing.json` (add explicit `fail_mode: "closed"` + `detector: {id_prefix: "rebar.builtin.security."}` to existing security rows).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the DET fallback descriptor field name: pick one name (`scenario` matches the live schema at registry.py:353) and use it consistently in both the 'What' section and the thin-slice step 2 description.
findings.finding: The field name for the detector rule message in the DET fallback descriptor is stated as `scenario` in the 'What' section but as `rubric` in the thin-slice sequencing step 2 (remediation, run 4). These are contradictory names for the same field, and the live descriptor schema uses `scenario` (registry.py:353). An implementer reading run 4 will implement `rubric` and diverge from the schema.
findings.location: 'What' section (DET fallback descriptor shape) vs 'Thin-slice sequencing + coherence' step 2 (remediation, run 4)
findings.suggested_fix: Use `scenario` throughout (matching the existing descriptor dict key at registry.py:353); remove the `rubric =` label from run 4 step 2.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the plan where in `_descriptor_from_prompt` the `exec == 'DET'` branch is inserted: confirm it must be an early-return BEFORE the `prompts.get_prompt(...)` call (line 344), reading only the routing entry for the descriptor fields.
findings.finding: `_descriptor_from_prompt` calls `prompts.get_prompt(...)` unconditionally at line 344 before any exec-tier branch. The plan says the DET branch 'skips `get_prompt`' but does not specify where in the function the `exec == 'DET'` early-return is inserted, nor what the fallback is if a DET routing entry's `exec` field is present but no prompt file exists (the intended case). This is a processing protocol co-location gap: the decision rule ('skip get_prompt for DET') is stated in the 'What' section but not anchored to the function's control flow.
findings.suggested_fix: After the routing lookup (line 345–347), add: `if routing.get('exec','').upper() == 'DET': return { 'id': cid, 'exec': 'DET', 'facet': routing.get('facet', cid), 'name': routing.get('name', cid), 'scenario': routing.get('detector', {}).get('id') or routing.get('detector', {}).get('id_prefix', ''), 'checklist': [], ... }` — before the `get_prompt` call at line 344.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the 'Data-driven map location' section exactly which `criteria_routing.json` file(s) gain `detector`/`fail_mode` schema fields: code-review's packaged file (security rows) and/or the `.rebar/` overlay only (project invariant rows), with an explicit statement that plan-review's packaged file is NOT modified.
findings.finding: The phrase 'move INTO `criteria_routing.json` (both gates)' in 'Data-driven map location' is ambiguous about whether the `detector`/`fail_mode` schema fields must be added to plan-review's packaged `criteria_routing.json` as well as code-review's, or only to code-review's (with plan-review reading them only via the `.rebar/` overlay). The Cross-story section clarifies security rows are already outside plan-review's CANONICAL_LLM, but does not explicitly state whether plan-review's packaged routing file needs the schema additions.
findings.location: 'Data-driven map location (clears the hardcoded-prefix AC)' section — 'The detector→criterion association + `fail_mode` move INTO `criteria_routing.json` (both gates)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'move INTO `criteria_routing.json` (both gates)' with: 'move INTO the packaged `code_review/criteria_routing.json` (security rows, explicit `fail_mode: closed`) and the per-repo `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` overlay (project DET rows, `fail_mode: open` default); plan-review's packaged `criteria_routing.json` is NOT modified by this story.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a checkboxed AC: 'run_security_detectors is renamed to run_detectors; a deprecated alias run_security_detectors is retained and a test asserts both return identical results.'
findings.finding: The run_security_detectors → run_detectors rename is a described deliverable (with alias and alias-equivalence test) but has no checkboxed AC verifying that (a) the rename is complete and (b) the alias is present and working. Without an AC, the alias may be omitted without failing the exit checklist.
findings.location: Schema + migration details — 'Rename' bullet; Acceptance Criteria sections (all rounds)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add named tests for `_validate_routing_entry` invalid-input paths: `fail_mode` outside `{open, closed}` (e.g., wrong case, unknown value), `detector` selector missing both `id` and `id_prefix`, and `detector` selector with both `id` and `id_prefix` (ambiguous); assert each raises a validation error rather than silently defaulting.
findings.finding: `_validate_routing_entry` is extended with two new validated fields (`fail_mode ∈ {open,closed}` and the `detector` selector shape `{id?, id_prefix?}`), but the plan names no tests for invalid/malformed inputs to these fields (e.g., `fail_mode: 'banana'`, selector with neither `id` nor `id_prefix`, or selector with both). Since `fail_mode:closed` enforcement is security-critical, a silent default on a malformed entry could silently revert a security-class criterion to fail-open.
findings.location: Schema + migration details section / `_validate_routing_entry` / AC: '`_validate_routing_entry` rejects a `fail_mode` outside `{open, closed}`'
findings.suggested_fix: In the testing-plan section, add: 'Tests for `_validate_routing_entry` invalid inputs: (a) `fail_mode` not in `{"open","closed"}` → `RoutingValidationError`; (b) `detector` selector with neither `id` nor `id_prefix` → error; (c) `detector` selector with both `id` and `id_prefix` → error. These run alongside the existing routing-validation test suite.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the `detector` selector validation rules in the schema details: state that exactly one of `id` or `id_prefix` is required (or document priority if both are allowed), and add a corresponding check to `_validate_routing_entry`.
findings.finding: The `detector` selector schema (`detector: {id?: str, id_prefix?: str}`) is defined in 'Schema + migration details' but the decision rules for invalid states are never specified: (a) what `_validate_routing_entry` does when both `id` and `id_prefix` are absent (is at least one required?); (b) what the consumer does when both are present (union? error?). These branching rules are needed by both the validator and the matching logic in the generalized scan consumer.
findings.location: 'Schema + migration details' subsection (remediation, run 4) — `detector` selector shape paragraph
findings.suggested_fix: Require exactly one of `id` or `id_prefix` (mutually exclusive). `_validate_routing_entry` raises `RegistryError` if both absent or both present.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that existing documentation/docstrings referencing run_security_detectors are either updated to run_detectors or explicitly noted as intentionally preserved (pointing to the alias) in the ADR or a code comment.
findings.finding: The plan renames run_security_detectors to run_detectors but does not state whether existing documentation or internal references (docstrings, README sections, architecture notes) that name run_security_detectors will be updated or deliberately left as-is (pointing to the alias). This may strand stale references in docs.
findings.location: Schema + migration details — 'Rename' bullet; Docs deliverable section
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the ADR or the rename bullet: 'Existing docstring/doc references to run_security_detectors are updated to run_detectors; the alias docstring marks it deprecated with a pointer to the new name.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
